frequency_range: 300-8,000+ Hz combined; sub-bass 50-200 Hz wind drone

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST MULTI-SPECIES SPS MAXIMUM ZONE ENTRY. All previous SPS E=8+ entries document single species or specific paired events. Degree 310 documents the simultaneous presence of 8-15+ species calling through a Pacific storm. A new entry type: the acoustic complex rather than the single acoustic event.
theory_nodes.description: First multi-species acoustic complex in the SPS maximum zone.

theory_nodes.concept: EXACT ENERGY MATCH with Mudhoney: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Only exact energy match in the Grunge Summit cluster. Both events consist of multiple simultaneous acoustic signals remaining individually identifiable within combined event. The layering principle: additive not assimilative.
theory_nodes.description: Exact E=8 energy match and structural parallel with Mudhoney's simultaneous guitar layering.

theory_nodes.concept: Pacific storm acoustic augmentation: rain on alder/maple canopy (40-80 dB white noise floor), wind in Sitka spruce/Douglas fir (50-200 Hz drone), humidity carry effects (mid-frequency calls carry further in humid air). Storm not silencing chorus but transforming it: volume selection mechanism amplifies loud callers.
theory_nodes.description: Pacific storm as acoustic transformer of the dawn chorus.

theory_nodes.concept: Individual species roles: American Robin (anchor, first singer), Pacific Wren (8-10g, 30+ syllable types, most complex), Song Sparrow (8-20 song repertoire, most variable), Swainson's Thrush (spiral upward song, storm trigger), Common Raven (200-1,500 Hz sub-bass anchor).
theory_nodes.description: Species roles in the storm dawn chorus acoustic architecture.

cross_references.relationship: Preceding degree at E=9. Single-species coordinated pair event (E=9) followed by multi-species storm complex (E=8). Acoustic complexity increases as energy decreases by one unit.
Succeeding degree at E=9. Multi-species terrestrial (E=8) immediately precedes marine multi-voice (E=9). Storm dawn chorus bridges between the two E=9 peaks of the Summit cluster.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Exact energy match. Multiple simultaneous guitar voices (Arm rhythm + Turner lead + Lukin bass) through the Sub Pop noise storm. Multiple simultaneous bird voices through the Pacific rain storm. Layering principle: individual identity preserved in simultaneous combination. The only exact energy match in the Grunge Summit.
